control_description: Role-based access control must be implemented for all financial systems. Access provisioning must follow a documented approval workflow with manager and system owner authorization.
Multi-factor authentication must be enforced for all remote access to financial systems and privileged accounts. MFA enrollment must be tracked and verified.
User access rights to financial systems must be reviewed quarterly by system owners. Reviews must document users reviewed, exceptions found, and remediation actions taken.
Privileged and administrative accounts must follow least-privilege principles. Emergency access must be time-limited and audited. Password policies must enforce complexity and rotation.
All changes to production financial systems must follow a documented change management workflow including request, review, approval, implementation, and post-implementation verification.
Change requestors must not approve their own changes. Development and production environments must be segregated. Emergency changes must have documented post-implementation review.
Month-end and quarter-end close processes must follow a documented checklist. All reconciliations must be completed, journal entries approved, and management sign-off obtained.
All journal entries must be reviewed and approved by authorized personnel before posting. Supporting documentation must be attached and retained.
Regular vulnerability assessments must be conducted on financial systems. Critical and high vulnerabilities must be remediated within defined SLAs. Scan results must be documented.
Business continuity and disaster recovery plans must be documented with defined RTO and RPO. DR tests must be conducted at least annually with results documented.
Automated backup procedures must be in place for all critical financial data. Backup integrity must be verified and restoration tested periodically.
Network segmentation must isolate financial system environments. Firewall rules must be documented and reviewed. Intrusion detection must be active on critical segments.
